report_name: Jscrambler NPM Package Hijacked in Supply Chain Attack, Deploys Infostealer Targeting Cloud & AI Dev Secrets

attack_patterns.name: Compromise Software Supply Chain
attack_patterns.description: The attacker compromised a maintainer's npm publishing token to publish malicious versions of the 'jscrambler' package to the public npm registry.

attack_patterns.name: Command and Scripting Interpreter
attack_patterns.description: The malicious npm package used a 'preinstall' hook in the package.json file to automatically execute a setup script that deployed an infostealer payload.

attack_patterns.name: Cloud Instance Metadata API
attack_patterns.description: The infostealer was designed to search for and exfiltrate cloud credentials for AWS, Azure, and Google Cloud from developer workstations and CI/CD environments.

attack_patterns.name: Credentials In Files
attack_patterns.description: The infostealer searched for and exfiltrated cryptocurrency wallet data from MetaMask, Phantom, and Exodus, as well as configuration files for AI coding tools.

attack_patterns.name: Scheduled Task
attack_patterns.description: On Windows systems, the infostealer attempted to establish persistence by creating a hidden scheduled task.

attack_patterns.name: Launch Agent
attack_patterns.description: On macOS systems, the infostealer used a LaunchAgent to establish persistence.

malware.name: Rust Infostealer
malware.description: A cross-platform infostealer written in Rust, distributed via malicious 'jscrambler' npm packages. It was designed to harvest sensitive developer credentials from cloud providers (AWS, Azure, GCP), cryptocurrency wallets, and AI coding tools.

tools.name: npm
tools.description: The npm package registry was used as the distribution vector for the malicious 'jscrambler' package versions containing the infostealer.

identities.name: Jscrambler
identities.description: The vendor whose 'jscrambler' npm package was hijacked in a software supply chain attack.

identities.name: npm
identities.description: The public package registry that hosted and distributed the malicious versions of the 'jscrambler' package.

identities.name: AWS
identities.description: Cloud provider whose developer credentials were targeted by the Rust infostealer.

identities.name: Azure
identities.description: Cloud provider whose developer credentials were targeted by the Rust infostealer.

identities.name: Google Cloud
identities.description: Cloud provider whose developer credentials were targeted by the Rust infostealer.

identities.name: MetaMask
identities.description: Cryptocurrency wallet whose data was targeted by the Rust infostealer.

identities.name: Phantom
identities.description: Cryptocurrency wallet whose data was targeted by the Rust infostealer.

identities.name: Exodus
identities.description: Cryptocurrency wallet whose data was targeted by the Rust infostealer.

identities.name: Claude Desktop
identities.description: AI coding tool whose configuration files and credentials were targeted by the Rust infostealer.

identities.name: Cursor
identities.description: AI coding tool whose configuration files and credentials were targeted by the Rust infostealer.

identities.name: VS Code
identities.description: Development environment whose configuration files were targeted by the Rust infostealer.

indicators.name: Jscrambler Supply Chain Attack Payload
indicators.x_netsecio_sigma_rule:
title: Jscrambler Supply Chain Attack Payload
id: 4f5a6b7c-8d9e-4f1a-b2c3-d4e5f6a7b8c9
status: experimental
description: Detects the creation of the malicious 'intro.js' payload from the compromised jscrambler npm package.
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-12
references:
  - https://socket.dev/blog/jscrambler-supply-chain-attack
tags:
  - attack.t1195.002
level: critical
logsource:
  category: file_event
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    TargetFilename|endswith: '\node_modules\jscrambler\intro.js'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the presence of the malicious payload file 'intro.js' dropped by compromised versions of the 'jscrambler' npm package.

indicators.name: Suspicious Jscrambler NPM Package Installation
indicators.pattern:
title: Suspicious Jscrambler NPM Package Installation
id: 1a2b3c4d-5e6f-4a7b-8c9d-0e1f2a3b4c5d
status: experimental
description: Detects the installation of specific malicious versions of the 'jscrambler' npm package via command line arguments.
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-12
references:
  - https://socket.dev/blog/jscrambler-supply-chain-attack
tags:
  - attack.t1195.002
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_base:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\npm.cmd'
      - '\node.exe'
    CommandLine|contains|all:
      - 'install'
      - 'jscrambler@'
  selection_versions:
    CommandLine|contains:
      - '8.14.0'
      - '8.16.0'
      - '8.17.0'
      - '8.18.0'
      - '8.20.0'
  condition: all of selection_*
indicators.description: Detects command-line execution of 'npm install' for known malicious versions of the 'jscrambler' package.

indicators.name: Suspicious Scheduled Task Creation by Node Process
indicators.pattern:
title: Suspicious Scheduled Task Creation by Node Process
id: 6f7a8b9c-0d1e-4f2a-b3c4-d5e6f7a8b9c0
status: experimental
description: Detects a scheduled task being created by a node.js or npm process, which could indicate persistence from a malicious npm package.
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-12
references:
  - https://socket.dev/blog/jscrambler-supply-chain-attack
tags:
  - attack.t1053.005
level: medium
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    ParentImage|endswith:
      - '\node.exe'
      - '\npm.cmd'
    Image|endswith: '\schtasks.exe'
    CommandLine|contains: '/create'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the creation of a Windows Scheduled Task by a node or npm process, a TTP used by the malicious jscrambler package for persistence.

indicators.name: Suspicious LaunchAgent Creation by Node Process
indicators.pattern:
title: Suspicious LaunchAgent Creation by Node Process
id: 7a8b9c0d-1e2f-4a3b-b4c5-d6e7f8a9b0c1
status: experimental
description: Detects a LaunchAgent .plist file being created by a node.js process, which could indicate persistence from a malicious npm package on macOS.
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-12
references:
  - https://socket.dev/blog/jscrambler-supply-chain-attack
tags:
  - attack.t1543.001
level: medium
logsource:
  category: file_event
  product: macos
detection:
  selection:
    TargetFilename|contains: '/Library/LaunchAgents/'
    TargetFilename|endswith: '.plist'
    Image|endswith: '/node'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the creation of a macOS LaunchAgent by a node process, a TTP used by the malicious jscrambler package for persistence.

relationships.source_name: Suspicious Scheduled Task Creation by Node Process
Suspicious LaunchAgent Creation by Node Process
